data.title: DeFi Platforms Urged to Address Security Risks Amid New Vulnerability
data.description: According to Odaily, 23pds, the Chief Information Security Officer at SlowMist Technology, has highlighted a significant security concern on the X platform. A new attack chain has emerged related to the latest remote code execution vulnerability in React/Next.js, which could substantially increase the success rate of attacks. Many DeFi platforms currently utilize React, making them susceptible to this vulnerability. It is crucial for these platforms to be vigilant about potential security risks.

data.title: Strategy's Bitcoin Holdings Unaffected by Share Price Drop, Says Bitwise CIO
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitwise chief investment officer Matt Hougan has dismissed concerns that Strategy (MSTR) would be compelled to sell its Bitcoin holdings if its share price declines. Hougan emphasized that the company's financial stability remains intact, despite market speculations suggesting otherwise. He highlighted that Strategy's chairman, Michael Saylor, maintains a strong belief in Bitcoin (BTC), and the company is not under immediate financial pressure to liquidate its assets.

Strategy's CEO, Phong Le, had previously mentioned the possibility of selling some Bitcoin as a "last resort" if the company's market value fell below its Bitcoin holdings. However, Hougan reassured that with no debt maturing until 2027 and sufficient cash reserves to cover interest payments, the likelihood of such a scenario is minimal. He noted that selling $60 billion worth of Bitcoin would significantly impact the market, equating it to two years of Bitcoin ETF inflows.

Despite facing a prolonged crypto market downturn and potential delisting from the MSCI stock market index, Hougan believes Strategy can endure the challenges. He pointed out that Bitcoin's current trading price is 24% above the average acquisition cost for Strategy, providing the company with a buffer even if its stock value dips below its net asset value (NAV). Hougan also mentioned that Strategy's financial obligations, including $800 million in annual interest payments, are manageable given its $1.4 billion cash reserve.

Over the past month, Strategy's stock has declined by 24.69%, closing at $186.01 on Friday. This decline may be partly attributed to MSCI's announcement in October, which suggested the potential exclusion of digital asset treasury companies with over 50% crypto assets from its index. Such a move could compel index-tracking funds to sell, exerting additional pressure on Strategy's stock price.

Hougan remains optimistic, arguing that historical data shows index changes often have a smaller impact than anticipated. He cited the example of Strategy's addition to the Nasdaq-100 Index last December, which required funds to purchase $2.1 billion of the stock with minimal effect on its price. Hougan's insights suggest that Strategy's current financial position and market dynamics may not significantly alter investor sentiment or the company's share price.

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs See Significant Inflows on December 4
data.description:
According to Foresight News, data from SoSoValue indicates that on December 4, XRP spot ETFs experienced a total net inflow of $12.84 million. The Franklin XRP ETF (XRPZ) led with a single-day net inflow of $5.7 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $132 million. Following closely, the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP) recorded a net inflow of $3.76 million, with its historical total reaching $185 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs stands at $881 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 0.69%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $887 million.

data.title: Data Breach at Lemon Cash Exposes User Information
data.description: According to Foresight News, Argentine cryptocurrency platform Lemon Cash has experienced a data breach due to a hack on its external analytics service provider, Mixpanel. The breach resulted in the exposure of some users' names and email addresses. Lemon Cash emphasized that its own systems were not compromised, and sensitive information such as users' private keys, mnemonic phrases, funds, and account balances remain secure. The company has notified affected users via email, advising them to be cautious of potential phishing attacks.

data.title: AI and Big Data Crypto Projects Ranked by Development Activity
data.description: According to PANews, Santiment has released data on the development activity of AI and big data-focused cryptocurrency projects over the past 30 days. The projects with the highest activity include Internet Computer (ICP), Chainlink (LINK), NEAR Protocol (NEAR), Oasis Network (ROSE), Filecoin (FIL), Livepeer (LPT), Recall (RECALL), The Graph (GRT), iExec (RLC), and Injective (INJ). ICP and LINK continue to lead the rankings, while Recall has shown significant improvement. In contrast, GRT, RLC, and INJ have experienced a decline in their positions. The rankings are based on GitHub development activity, indicating the level of technical investment in AI and big data by these projects.

data.title: EU Commission Allegedly Plans Significant Fine Against X Company
data.description: According to Foresight News, U.S. Vice President Vance has tweeted about rumors that the European Commission intends to impose a substantial fine on X Company for not participating in a review process. Vance criticized the move, stating that the EU should support freedom of speech rather than targeting American businesses over trivial matters.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences $267 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market witnessed liquidations totaling $267 million in the past 24 hours. Long positions accounted for $180 million, while short positions saw liquidations of $87.3 million. Specifically, Bitcoin long positions faced liquidations of $60.86 million, and Bitcoin short positions were liquidated for $29.64 million. Ethereum long positions experienced liquidations amounting to $55.94 million, with Ethereum short positions seeing $30.11 million in liquidations.

In the same period, a total of 92,772 traders were liquidated globally. The largest single liquidation occurred on Hyperliquid's BTC-USD pair, valued at $5.31 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Signals Bear Market: One Thing Could Flip It, Says CryptoQuant CEO
data.description:
Bitcoin may be sliding into a new bear phase unless fresh macro liquidity – particularly through spot ETFs – returns to the market, according to CryptoQuant CEO Ki Young Ju. Bitcoin Bear Market Incoming? Sharing a composite on-chain dashboard overlaid on the BTC price, Ju wrote on X: “Most Bitcoin on-chain indicators are bearish. Without macro liquidity, we enter a bear cycle. ” The chart stacks ten CryptoQuant metrics behind the price in a red-to-green heatmap from 2021 to 2025, highlighting how regime shifts in prior cycles coincided with clusters of bearish 
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-bear-market-one-thing-flip-it-cryptoquant-ceo/
